source: NTLA-2001 MAGNITUDE trial, FDA 2024 Gene Editing Guidance
description: Gene editing-specific expected effects when delivered by LNP/mRNA. Inherits full LNP/mRNA profile (~14 entries) plus editing-specific additions. Key advantage: transient Cas9 expression from mRNA (hours to days, not months) limits cumulative off-target exposure vs AAV delivery.

expected_findings.rationale: Successful gene knockout/correction. E.g., 52-96% TTR reduction (NTLA-2001).

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: 58-79% prevalence in humans; universal in NHP
expected_findings.rationale: Prior S. aureus/S. pyogenes exposure. Lower concern than AAV delivery due to transient Cas9 expression from mRNA.
Memory T cells from prior bacterial exposure. Lower risk than AAV delivery — transient mRNA-expressed Cas9 is present hours to days, not months.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Selection of p53-null clones = oncogenic concern
expected_findings.rationale: DSB-induced DNA damage response. Nuclease editors only.
NHEJ between multiple DSB sites. Nuclease editors only.

expected_findings.description: Large deletions (>1 kb) at DSB sites
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Deletion of tumor suppressor or essential gene = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Error-prone DSB repair. Nuclease editors only.

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: Hundreds per genome for rAPOBEC1-based CBEs
expected_findings.severity_threshold: SNVs in tumor suppressor genes = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: APOBEC deaminase activity on single-stranded DNA. Base editor-specific (not nuclease or prime editor).
